If 'Use Same as Storage' is enabled, the social article object storage configuration will be used.

aiPriceHub.step2Desc: Select pricing collection targets to sync

strategy.title: Strategy
strategy.subtitle: Choose how to select among multiple enabled global configs, and how failures are handled.
strategy.multiModelNote: When multiple global configs are enabled (by order), the following applies:
strategy.failureBehaviorFirst: First: Always use the first active config (lowest order). If that call fails, the request fails immediately — no automatic fallback to the next config.
strategy.failureBehaviorRoundRobin: Round-robin: Each new request uses the next config in order. If a call fails, that request fails; the next request will use the next config in the rotation.

config.title: Configs
config.subtitle: Global and user-level model settings
config.perUserDesc: User-specific configs override global. List below.
config.noUsersHint: No users. Cannot add per-user config.
config.testPromptPlaceholder: Enter a prompt to send to the model…
config.streamingParamHint: Request param stream: use SSE stream for incremental response.
config.noGlobalConfigs: No global configs. Add one below.

errors.invalid_api_key: Invalid or expired API key. Please check and try again.
errors.auth_failed: Authentication failed. Please check API key or account permissions.
errors.rate_limit: Rate limit or quota exceeded. Please try again later.
errors.not_found: Model or endpoint not found. Please check model name and API base URL.
errors.timeout: Connection timed out. Please check network and API base URL.
errors.network_error: Cannot reach the service. Please check API base URL and network.
errors.permission_denied: Access denied. Please check API key and account permissions.
errors.unknown: Connection test failed. Please check your configuration and try again.

dataSettings.title: Scheduled Tasks
dataSettings.subtitle: Data retention, cleanup and aggregation schedules for LLM usage.
dataSettings.retentionDesc: Keep usage and series records for this many days; older data is deleted by the cleanup task.
dataSettings.cleanupDesc: Run scheduled cleanup of old LLM usage and series data.
dataSettings.aggregationCrontabDesc: One task aggregates hour, day and month series each run.
dataSettings.crontabHelp: 5 fields: minute hour day month weekday

list.subtitle: View and filter task execution records
list.searchPlaceholder: Search by task name or module...

stats.chartByTaskNameSubtitle: Top task types by total count

settings.title: Task Settings
settings.subtitle: Configurable task timeout and retention
settings.moduleConfigSubtitle: Set default rules and parameters for core functions.
settings.timeoutDesc: Tasks running longer than the configured minutes are marked as failed (by scheduled job).
settings.retentionDesc: The number of days completed/failed records are kept before cleanup.
settings.cleanupDesc: Automatically clean up old execution records, runs daily by default.
settings.markTimeoutDesc: Regularly check and mark tasks that exceed the time limit.
settings.crontabHelp: 5 fields: minute hour day month weekday, e.g. 0 2 * * *
settings.configNote: Settings below are configurable; save to apply.

stats.title: Send Statistics
stats.subtitle: Monitor send trends, success rate and channel distribution
stats.seriesSubtitle: Send volume by hour / day / month

records.title: Send Records
records.subtitle: Notification send records with filters and pagination

channels.title: Notification Channels
channels.subtitle: Manage notification channels (Webhook and email SMTP); add multiple and set default.
channels.noChannels: No channels yet. Click Add Channel to create one.
channels.name: Name
channels.orderDesc: The active channel with the smallest order value is used for sending; smaller value = higher priority, same value uses creation time.
channels.messagePrefixDesc: Prepended to the message body when sending; some platforms require a source identifier.
channels.signSecretPlaceholder: Optional; required when Feishu bot has signature verification enabled
channels.signSecretDesc: When Feishu custom bot has signature verification enabled, paste the secret here; requests will be signed automatically.
channels.signSecretEditHint: Leave blank when editing to keep current secret
channels.mergeEnabledDesc: Merge multiple notifications with the same key within the time window.
channels.mergeWindowMinutesDesc: 0 = no merge; use with Enable merge when > 0.
channels.editSaveHint: You can save directly if nothing was changed; after changing config, validate first then save.
channels.smtpPasswordEditHint: Leave blank when editing to keep current password
channels.fromNameDesc: Display name shown to recipients; if empty, only the email address is shown.
channels.subjectPrefixDesc: Prepended to the email subject when sending, to identify the source.
channels.validateEmailDialogDesc: Enter the recipient email to receive a test message, or leave empty to only test SMTP connection.
channels.testRecipientEmailDesc: Optional. If filled, a test email will be sent to this address; otherwise only SMTP connection is tested.
channels.silenceWindowMinutesDesc: Same alert will not be sent again within this period (only once); 0 means no silence.
channels.isActiveDesc: When off, this channel will not be used for sending.
channels.setDefaultDesc: Use this channel when no channel is specified.

settings.title: Notification Settings
settings.subtitle: Notification module schedule and cleanup config (admin)
settings.retentionDesc: Keep notification records for this many days; cleanup task deletes older ones.
settings.cleanupDesc: Run cleanup at the cron schedule below to remove expired records.
settings.crontabHelp: Five-field cron: minute hour day month weekday, e.g. 0 2 * * * = daily at 02:00.

config.title: Notification Config
config.subtitle: Webhook and notification channel config (admin)
config.webhookConfigDesc: Configure Feishu, WeCom, etc. webhooks for system notifications.

share.viewExpired: This share link has expired or been removed

scheduledTasks.title: Scheduled Tasks
scheduledTasks.subtitle: Manage and configure scheduled tasks
scheduledTasks.name: Name
scheduledTasks.deleteMessage: Are you sure you want to delete this scheduled task?
scheduledTasks.deleteNotSupported: Deleting task records is not supported yet.
scheduledTasks.batchDeleteMessage: Are you sure you want to delete {count} selected scheduled tasks?
scheduledTasks.batchToggleSuccess: Successfully toggled status for {count} scheduled tasks
scheduledTasks.collectStarted: Collection task started, redirecting to tasks list...
scheduledTasks.collectErrorNoParams: This scheduled task has no collection parameters (keywords or topics) configured
scheduledTasks.collectAlreadyRunning: Collection task is already running, please wait for the current task to complete
scheduledTasks.githubTokenNotConfigured: GitHub Token is not configured. Please configure it in Settings page and try again.
scheduledTasks.onlyAdminCanCollectGitHub: Only admin users can create GitHub collection tasks.

form.name: Task Name
form.taskTypeHelp: Select the type of scheduled task to execute
form.minuteHelp: Minute (0-59), comma-separated for multiple, * for every minute
form.hourHelp: Hour (0-23), comma-separated for multiple, * for every hour
form.dayOfMonthHelp: Day of month (1-31), comma-separated for multiple, * for every day
form.dayOfMonthInvalid: Day of month must be a number between 1-31, comma-separated for multiple
form.monthOfYearHelp: Month (1-12), comma-separated for multiple, * for every month
form.monthOfYearInvalid: Month must be a number between 1-12, comma-separated for multiple
form.dayOfWeekHelp: Day of week (0-7, both 0 and 7 mean Sunday), comma-separated for multiple, * for every week
form.dayOfWeekInvalid: Day of week must be a number between 0-7, comma-separated for multiple
form.timezoneDescription: Tasks will be scheduled according to your local timezone ({timezone})
form.socialAccountHelp: Used to distinguish article storage paths when publishing to different social media accounts (e.g., toutiao_ai, weibo_user)
form.transformFormatsHelp: Select one or more formats to generate for published articles
form.transformFormatsRequired: Please select at least one publish format
form.keywordsOrTopicsRequired: Please provide at least keywords or topics
form.keywordsOrTopicsOnlyOne: You can only select either keywords or topics, not both
form.atLeastOneKeyword: Please enter at least one keyword
form.maxProjectsHelp: Maximum number of projects to collect (default: 20, max: 100). Actual count may be less as GitHub trending page has a fixed number of projects
form.minuteInvalid: Minute must be a number between 0-59, comma-separated for multiple
form.hourInvalid: Hour must be a number between 0-23, comma-separated for multiple
form.alertTaskMinuteHelp: Alert task restriction: Only a single minute value (0-59) is allowed, e.g., 5 means execute at the 5th minute of every hour
form.alertTaskHourHelp: Alert task restriction: Must execute every hour, only */1 or * is allowed
form.alertTaskMinuteInvalid: Alert tasks can only set a single minute value (0-59), wildcards, ranges, or comma-separated values are not allowed
form.alertTaskHourInvalid: Alert tasks must execute every hour, hour field can only be set to */1 or *
form.startTimeHelp: Task will start from this time, default is current time
form.previewEveryHour: Execute at {minute} minutes past every hour
form.previewScheduleWithDay: Execute on day {dayOfMonth} at {minute} minutes past {hours}
form.previewScheduleWithMonth: Execute in month {monthOfYear} at {minute} minutes past {hours}
form.previewScheduleFull: Execute on day {dayOfMonth} of month {monthOfYear}, on {dayOfWeek}, at {minute} minutes past {hours}
form.enabledHelp: When enabled, the task will automatically execute according to the schedule rules
form.nameAutoGenerated: Task name will be auto-generated based on selected parameters
form.nameAutoGeneratedHelp: Task name will be automatically generated based on keywords, topics, region, language and schedule time
form.startTimeDisplayHelp: Start time defaults to current time, task will start from this time

dashboardPage.title: Monitoring cockpit
dashboardPage.subtitle: Track tenant, host, license and collection task health in one place, then handle high-risk sources first.
dashboardPage.scopeHint: Review core monitoring metrics within the current data source scope
dashboardPage.recentTasksSubtitle: Review failed runs and unusually slow collection jobs first.
dashboardPage.riskSubtitle: Compress the most important risk counts into one glance.
dashboardPage.failedTasksSubtitle: Number of failed runs in the last 7 days
dashboardPage.invalidLicenseHostsSubtitle: Review license validity and sync status first

tenantsPage.title: Tenant management
tenantsPage.subtitle: Inspect tenant status, host scale and license capacity usage by data source.
tenantsPage.scopeHint: Review tenant status and capacity distribution within the current data source scope
tenantsPage.activeTenantsSubtitle: Tenants in a healthy state within the current list
tenantsPage.hostFleetSubtitle: Total hosts covered by the current tenant list
tenantsPage.remainingLicensesSubtitle: License capacity still available across the current tenants
tenantsPage.filtersSubtitle: Find tenants quickly by name while keeping the current data source scope.

licensesPage.title: License management
licensesPage.subtitle: Review capacity usage, expiration and owning tenant to spot high-consumption or exhausted resources.
licensesPage.scopeHint: Review license capacity and utilization within the current data source scope
licensesPage.catalogSubtitle: Number of license records in the current list
licensesPage.utilizationSubtitle: Usage ratio aggregated from the current list
licensesPage.exhaustedSubtitle: Licenses with no remaining capacity in the current list
licensesPage.filtersSubtitle: Narrow by type and capacity status to focus on risky licenses first.

hostsPage.title: Host management
hostsPage.subtitle: Review host status, boot health, license validity and tenant ownership, then fix unhealthy assets first.
hostsPage.scopeHint: Review host health, authorization and error signals within the current data source scope
hostsPage.totalSubtitle: All hosts in the current data source
hostsPage.healthySubtitle: Healthy hosts in the current data source
hostsPage.errorSubtitle: Hosts with error output in the current data source
hostsPage.invalidLicenseSubtitle: Hosts with invalid licenses in the current data source
hostsPage.summaryHint: These totals always reflect every host in the current data source. Click a card to update the list filters below.
hostsPage.filtersSubtitle: Narrow the list by name and task status to find risky hosts faster.

exportScopes.filteredHint: Keep the current data source and active filters when exporting.
exportScopes.dataSourceAllHint: Ignore local page filters and export every host in the selected data source.

hostsPage.activeFiltersSubtitle: Filters currently applied to the list
hostsPage.listSubtitle: Primary facts first, anomalies on the right, so the table is easier to scan.
hostsPage.detailSubtitle: Inspect host metadata, runtime state, resource authorization and error output.

tasksPage.title: Task management
tasksPage.subtitle: Inspect collection execution history and trigger new sync jobs by data source.

tasksPage.runningSubtitle: Collection jobs still in progress in the current list

tasksPage.filtersSubtitle: Filter by execution state to focus on failed, running or pending jobs.

taskDetailPage.title: Task detail
taskDetailPage.subtitle: Inspect execution time, collected totals and error output.

settingsPage.systemSectionSubtitle: Saved values apply to new collection tasks and mail delivery.
